objective: Finish the dawn sanitation pass and keep the train dispatchable.
opposition: Heat surge, backed-up blackwater, and intake volume.
turn: Marisol spots a detainee with Gabriel's eyebrow scar in the intake queue.
consequence_cost: She hides her reaction and fails to report a mis-scanned wristband.

objective: Confirm the detainee's identity without flagging a family connection.
opposition: Locked manifest access and Teresa's remote oversight.
turn: The file identifies him as Rafael Avelar bound for Santa Avelina.
consequence_cost: Marisol leaves an unauthorized duplicate trace in the system.

objective: Get close enough to signal help before the car seals.
opposition: Security timing and Gabriel's placement under guard.
turn: Gabriel recognizes her and chooses to act as if she is a stranger.
consequence_cost: The train is still on schedule and Marisol has no private alliance.

objective: Move Gabriel into the trauma room on a medical pretext.
opposition: Guard suspicion and overloaded intake staff.
turn: Marisol sees his bruises and the bag holding his confiscated recorder.
consequence_cost: She burns medical goodwill and exposes personal urgency.

objective: Persuade Gabriel to let her fix the paperwork quietly.
opposition: Gabriel's fury and years of mistrust.
turn: He says she taught the line how to hide people once the blood is scrubbed away.
consequence_cost: Their estrangement becomes active resistance to her plan.

objective: Act normal through a layover meal while seeking a clerical correction.
opposition: Friendly routine, compliance stonewalling, and the need to mask panic.
turn: The hotline denies any pre-transfer remedy and Marisol pockets Gabriel's recorder from property storage.
consequence_cost: She crosses from private worry into secret evidence-taking.

objective: Find a lawful way to contest Gabriel's destination before departure.
opposition: Ancestral-admissibility rules and sealed digital records.
turn: Marisol learns the paternal certificate locks the case until after transfer.
consequence_cost: Official rescue becomes impossible on the line's timeline.

objective: Push Teresa for an exception.
opposition: Teresa's contract logic and implied threats around housing and rank.
turn: Teresa warns her to keep family feeling out of custody records.
consequence_cost: Marisol is formally watched and informally cornered.

objective: Tell Gabriel what she has learned and get him to trust any next step.
opposition: His refusal to plead and a guard's short time window.
turn: Gabriel hands her a scrap of other names tied to false-country assignments.
consequence_cost: Marisol's problem expands from one son to a pattern.

objective: Buy time before the southbound departure.
opposition: Auditable thresholds and dispatch pressure.
turn: Marisol orders a fabricated deep clean on Car 8.
consequence_cost: Crew overtime spikes and suspicion falls on maintenance.

objective: Hide the hold's real purpose and decide who else must know.
opposition: Ivo's questions and Gabriel's contempt.
turn: Ivo covers with a fake valve fault while Gabriel passes bunk numbers linked to false papers.
consequence_cost: Ivo is implicated and Gabriel forces a collective frame onto the sabotage.

objective: Decide whether to keep sabotaging after the first delay.
opposition: Night outage, stalled metal, and recorder testimony she cannot unhear.
turn: The recorded voices turn paperwork into named people with objects, accents, and destinations assigned by force.
consequence_cost: Marisol gives up the fantasy of saving only Gabriel.

objective: Survive Teresa's audit without exposing the delay as sabotage.
opposition: Log discrepancies, missing time, and Teresa's authority.
turn: Teresa downgrades Marisol's passcard and transfers Gabriel to a higher-security car.
consequence_cost: Marisol loses reach and future access.

objective: Keep Paloma clear of the widening investigation.
opposition: Injuries in the trauma room and security review of medication use.
turn: Paloma falsifies a pain-med timestamp to protect an injured detainee.
consequence_cost: She becomes complicit and vulnerable.

objective: Hold the crew together after the audit.
opposition: Housing threats, lost overtime rumors, and growing suspicion.
turn: Workers start treating any delay as a threat to their own survival.
consequence_cost: Marisol sees that sabotage may isolate her from labor as well as management.

objective: Get detainees through biometric rescan without force.
opposition: Guard pressure and rising collective fear.
turn: Naila starts a seated refusal in the aisle.
consequence_cost: Processing halts and security posture hardens.

objective: Prevent the restraint team from charging the car.
opposition: Teresa's order to clear the aisle and the guards' impatience.
turn: Marisol stalls long enough for the refusal to spread across bunks.
consequence_cost: Her hesitation becomes visible insubordination.

objective: Keep Gabriel and the weakest passengers from taking the blow.
opposition: Batons, cramped space, and panic.
turn: A young mother's wrist breaks and Naila is beaten despite the delay.
consequence_cost: Marisol's half-measure injures the people least able to absorb it.

objective: Salvage any trust after the crackdown.
opposition: Blood, screams, and Gabriel's rage.
turn: Gabriel tells her delayed cruelty is still cruelty when the line keeps control.
consequence_cost: Marisol understands timing alone will not undo the institution.

objective: Wait out the storm hold and decide whether to ask Ivo for more.
opposition: Exhaustion and their unresolved intimacy.
turn: Old tenderness resurfaces over cafeteria stew and cigarettes.
consequence_cost: Emotional entanglement returns to an already dangerous plan.

objective: Get Ivo to consider a larger role.
opposition: His pension cliff and child-support burden.
turn: He admits the route's failure could ruin him and still agrees to hear her out.
consequence_cost: Marisol now knows exactly what her sabotage could cost an ally.

objective: Decode what the recorder and bunk tags actually reveal.
opposition: Missing pages and Marisol's limited view of detainee coordination.
turn: Naila explains how medical deferrals make people vanish before transfer.
consequence_cost: The scope of the crime widens beyond wrongful destination paperwork.

objective: Verify the rumor inside company records.
opposition: Sealed clinic logs and looming record purge.
turn: Marisol finds sanitation holds matched to municipal labor invoices.
consequence_cost: The concession's true profit model becomes legible.

objective: Get evidence off company systems before it disappears.
opposition: Monitored network traffic and chain-of-custody restrictions.
turn: She leaks copied logs through a church archive tied to Paper House.
consequence_cost: Future breaches will now read as intentional sabotage.

objective: Identify a stoppage plan that management cannot quietly spin away.
opposition: Too many moving parts and low trust among allies.
turn: Marisol, Paloma, and Ivo isolate the few failures that legally force a public halt.
consequence_cost: The conspiracy becomes concrete and therefore fragile.

objective: Secure Gabriel's participation without promising a private escape.
opposition: His refusal to leave others behind and the history between them.
turn: They agree on visibility over rescue: he will organize names if she can force public witness.
consequence_cost: Both tie themselves to a collective outcome instead of family exception.

objective: Trigger a quarantine before quarterly reconciliation locks the files.
opposition: Surveillance and the need for perfect timing.
turn: Paloma introduces preserved biohazard samples into the audit path.
consequence_cost: The plan becomes irreversible once the sample trail exists.

objective: Hold the train long enough for outside inspectors to take over.
opposition: Teresa's rush to reroute and bury the discrepancy.
turn: Internal security traces the mismatch back toward the trauma room.
consequence_cost: Paloma is marked as the likely breach point.

objective: Keep detainees alive during the rushed transfer.
opposition: Heat, broken ventilation, and panicked staff.
turn: An elderly detainee collapses and dies mid-move.
consequence_cost: The sabotage gains an irreversible human cost.

objective: Keep the alliance from shattering after the death.
opposition: Grief, blame, and internal interviews.
turn: Gabriel says the dead now belong partly to Marisol's ledger too.
consequence_cost: Paloma is suspended and the conspiracy nearly breaks apart.

objective: Get Gabriel off the line during the blackout.
opposition: Track lockdown and identity checks.
turn: Marisol moves him through the sanitation corridor in coveralls to Retiro Sur.
consequence_cost: She becomes a fugitive from her own employer.

objective: Decide whether to stay hidden or go back for the others.
opposition: The rusting hideout and the truth neither wants to speak.
turn: Marisol admits she once scrubbed a restraint death scene before investigators arrived, and Gabriel says he has known for years.
consequence_cost: Excuse and fantasy die, leaving only responsibility.

objective: Re-enter the system without immediate arrest.
opposition: Management suspicion and reduced credentials.
turn: Teresa lets Marisol back under escort because she wants to make a deal.
consequence_cost: Marisol is inside the system again but under tighter control.

objective: Hear Teresa's bargain and test its price.
opposition: Gabriel's life and Teresa's precise family leverage.
turn: Teresa offers inland release for Gabriel in exchange for names and public loyalty.
consequence_cost: Private salvation becomes concretely available.

objective: Decide whether to take the bargain.
opposition: Belongings bins waiting for incineration and the memory of unnamed detainees.
turn: Marisol sees children's shoes and labeled hearing aids reduced to inventory.
consequence_cost: She chooses public ruin over private pardon.

objective: Run the humane investor demo while loading hidden failures into it.
opposition: Optics management, journalists, ministers, and security sweeps.
turn: Audio bleed, mistranslated menus, and a foul VIP lavatory make the polished show buckle.
consequence_cost: Once the performance starts collapsing there is no clean retreat.

objective: Get every sabotage element into position before the public station stop.
opposition: Time, spot checks, and fraying nerves.
turn: Ivo slows the coupling sequence just enough to align cars, witnesses, and stoppage triggers.
consequence_cost: He stays exposed in the danger zone too long.

objective: Turn a staged platform pause into undeniable public witness.
opposition: Teresa's live script and officers ready to clear the scene.
turn: Gabriel hijacks the carriage audio while Naila leads detainees in throwing assigned-country tags onto the platform.
consequence_cost: Secrecy breaks in front of civilians.

objective: Force municipal jurisdiction before security can reseal the train.
opposition: Locked purge controls and escalating force.
turn: Marisol opens the greywater valve, sending sewage and medical waste into public view.
consequence_cost: Security tackles her and Ivo's foot is crushed in the scramble.

objective: Make the stoppage stick beyond one scandalous minute.
opposition: Emergency spin, arrests, and terrified witnesses.
turn: Paloma hands preserved logs to municipal health inspectors as cameras roll.
consequence_cost: The conspiracy is exposed and everyone involved pays openly.

objective: Face the fallout without reducing it to strategy.
opposition: Hospital bureaucracy, pain, and guilt.
turn: Ivo refuses heroic framing and demands that workers' coercion be told too.
consequence_cost: Marisol's testimony must implicate the system that fed them all.

objective: Spend time with Gabriel before hearings separate them again.
opposition: Provisional release limits and years of damaged language.
turn: Over soup and bakery rolls they speak plainly, and Gabriel chooses to testify instead of vanish.
consequence_cost: Their reunion remains partial and legally exposed.

objective: Decide whether to narrow the deposition into a cleaner, safer story.
opposition: Defense counsel, institutional self-protection, and the temptation of selective truth.
turn: Marisol names the exact mechanisms by which she kept the trains clean and later broke them while Gabriel reads salvaged names outside in the first rain.
consequence_cost: The line loses its concession, but the wider regime begins adapting around the wound.
